DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO PLAINTIFF CHRISTINA FERNANDEZ'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND FOR SANCTIONS, THE COURT AKES THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. ON 8/31/17, AFTER THE HEARING WAS HELD, JUDGE PRO TEM DIANNE PEEBLES RULES AS FOLLOWS AS TO THE PREVIOUSLY SUBMITTED MATTER: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED AS TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENT NO. 4 ONLY AND THE DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO SUBMIT A VERIFIED RESPONSE THERETO BY SEPTEMBER 11, 2017. SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. PLAINTIFF IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE PRO TEM: DIANNE PEEBLES, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SEPHORA USA, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PREGNANCY DISCRIMINATION WHICH ALLEGES TWO CLAIMS, ONE CLAIM FOR FAILURE TO PROMOTE AND FOR TERMINATION OF EMPLOYMENT; SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED IN FAVOR OF SEPHORA FOR FAILURE TO PROMOTE AND DENIED AS FOR TERMINATION. THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FAILURE TO TAKE ALL REASONABLE STEPS NECESSARY TO PREVENT DISCRIMINATION AND HARASSMENT FROM OCCURING DOES NOT EXIST, TO THE EXTENT THIS IS A DISCRIMINATION CASE, IT FOLLOWS THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION WHICH IS GRANTED AS TO PROMOTE AND DENIED AS TO TERMINATION. THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR WRONGFUL TERMINATION, IN VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY WHICH FOLLOWS THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IS DENIED; THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION ON THE ISSUE REGARDING IIED IS GRANTED BECAUSE SEPHORA HAVE MET ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION AND AS TO THE OTHER ISSUE, PLAINTIFF IS NOT PRESENTING ANY TRIABLE DISPUTE WITH REGARD TO EXTREME AND OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT; THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION REGARDING DISABILITY DISCRIMINATION IS GRANTED; THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION REGARDING THE UNLAWFUL DENIAL OF REASONABLE ACCOMODATION FOR DISABILITY/FAILURE TO ENGAGE IN THE INTERACTIVE PROCESS IS GRANTED IN FAVOR OF SEPHORA. THE REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS GRANTED IN FAVOR OF SEPHORA. DEFEDANT'S COUNSEL TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/HK

Sephora Usa, Inc.'S Motion To Compel (1)Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One; And (2) Responses To Document Requests, Set Two; And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-09-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Ashwin Ladva of the Ladva Law Firm, Tel. No. 1-415-296-8844 appearing for
the Plaintiff;
Atty. Kourosh Jahansouz of Orrick Heerington & Sutcliffe LLP, Tel. No. 1-415-
773-5951 appearing for the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; Sephora Usa, Inc.'S Motion To Compel (1)Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One; And (2) Responses To Document Requests, Set Two; And (3) For Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED in part. The court modified its tentative ruling as to Document Request No. 5, a very limited subset will need to be provided as stated on the record and Plaintiff's response do not need to include information that Sephora can glean directly from the medical records. Defendant also withdraws responses to Special interrogatories 1, 2 and 3. While the issue is close, the court concludes that CCP 2016.060 extends the discovery cutoff date from a Saturday to a Monday where the trial date is on a Monday. Much of the discovery propounded by Sephora is fairly characterized as contention discovery which has limited but not no utility when served at the end of the discovery period. Most of the discovery requests are sufficiently narrowly tailored to seek only discovery relevant information whose likely probative value outweighs the burden on Ms. Fernandez to provide the information at this late stage of the case. Ms. Fernandez is not required to provide further response to the discovery requests that are not so narrowly tailored or seek information whose likely probative value is outweighed by the burden on Ms. Fernandez to provide the information at this late stage of the case. No later than August 20, 2018 plaintiff Christina Fernandez is required to provide verified code-compliant further responses to special interrogatories 5-10, 20 and 23 and document request 6 and produce all responsive documents in her possession, custody and control. Unless Ms. Fernandez states in writing that neither she nor any witness called by her will testify about her suffering from postpartum depression while she was on leave from Sephora or after the termination of her employment by Sephora, Ms. Fernandez is also required to provide verified code-compliant further responses to special interrogatories 13-19 and 22 and document requests 1-2 and produce all responsive documents in her possession, custody and control. Ms. Fernandez is not required to provide any further responses to special interrogatories 4, 11-12, 21, and 24 and document requests 3-5. Because Ms. Fernandez's position regarding the timeliness of the discovery requests did not lack substantial justification, Sephora's request for monetary sanctions is denied. Defendant's counsel to prepare form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR # 8600 =302/hk

Defendant Sephora Usa Inc.'S Motion For Order Compelling Complaince With Business Records Subpoena (Added Per Ex Parte Order Filed 8/1/18)

Atty. Ashwin Ladva of the Ladva Law Firm, Tel. No. 1-415-296-8844 appearing for
the Plaintiff;

773-5951 appearing for the Defendant.

RULING - The matter was not argued. Defendant Sephora Usa Inc.'S Motion For Order Compelling Complaince With Business Records Subpoena to nonparty Kaiser Permanente is GRANTED. No later than August 16, 2018, Kaiser is required to comply with the subpoena except that Kaiser should not produce any documents for any time period August 14, 2015 or earlier per the parties' prior agreement. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR # 8600 =302/hk

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jul-25-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. John F. Henning III of Henning & Wallace, Tel. No. 1-707-243-3522 specially
appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Chris P. Chang of Ladva Law Firm, Tel. No. 1-415-296-8844 appearing for the
Plaintiff;
Atty. Andrew R. Livingston & Atty. Kourash Jahansouz of Orrick Law Firm, Tel.
No. 1-415-773-5588 appearing for the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT SEPHORA USA, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication - Summary Adjudication as to the first cause of action for Pregnancy Discrimination which alleges two claims, one claim for failure to promote and for termination of employment; Summary adjudication is granted in favor of Sephora for failure to promote and denied as for termination. The second cause of action for failure to take all reasonable steps necessary to prevent discrimination and harassment from occuring does not exist, to the extent this is a discrimination case, it follows the first cause of action which is granted as to promote and denied as to termination. The third cause of action for wrongful termination, in violation of public policy which follows the first cause of action is denied; The Fourth cause of action on the issue regarding IIED is granted because Sephora have met its burden of production and as to the other issue, plaintiff is not presenting any triable dispute with regard to extreme and outrageous conduct; The Fifth cause of action regarding Disability discrimination is granted; The Sixth cause of action regarding the Unlawful Denial of Reasonable Accomodation for Disability/Failure to engage in the Interactive Process is granted in favor of Sephora. The request for punitive damages is granted in favor of Sephora. Defedant's counsel to prepare form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk
